name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America is one of the largest US short-term lenders, offering payday loans, installment loans, and lines of credit to borrowers with poor or no credit in 27 states.
description_long:
Founded in 1997 and headquartered in Spartanburg, South Carolina, Advance America is among the largest non-bank short-term consumer lenders in the United States. The company operates under the Purpose Financial parent entity and has been owned by Mexican conglomerate Grupo Elektra since its April 2012 acquisition for approximately $780 million. Advance America is a member of the Community Financial Services Association of America (CFSA) and holds state licenses in each of the 27 states where it operates. It earned BBB accreditation in September 2024 and currently holds an A+ BBB rating.

Advance America's core products are payday loans, installment loans, lines of credit, cash advances, and title loans — the last offered via a partnership with LoanCenter. Payday loans are short-term instruments (typically 2–4 weeks) charged at roughly $15 per $100 borrowed, translating to APRs ranging from 143.29% to 688.28% depending on state regulations. Installment loans are repaid over multiple scheduled payments but can still carry APRs exceeding 200%. The company explicitly targets borrowers with poor, thin, or no credit history who cannot qualify for conventional bank financing. Applications are accepted both online at advanceamerica.net and in person at 800–1,200+ retail storefronts nationwide, with same-day funding available for approvals submitted before 10:30am ET.

What sets Advance America apart operationally is its scale and dual-channel accessibility. With over 800 retail locations across 27 states, it offers walk-in access to emergency credit in markets where many competitors are online-only. The same-day funding capability is a genuine differentiator for borrowers in acute cash emergencies. Its long operating history since 1997 and CFSA membership signal a degree of regulatory engagement uncommon among smaller payday lenders. Revenue is reported at approximately $1.7 billion, reflecting the company's dominant position in the subprime short-term lending segment.

Honestly assessed, Advance America fills a real market gap for underserved borrowers but comes with serious cost tradeoffs. APRs reaching 688.28% make these among the most expensive forms of consumer credit available, and the debt cycle risk is well-documented in this product category. Despite the A+ BBB rating, the company's BBB customer review score sits at just 1.7 out of 5 based on 154 reviews, with 302 complaints filed in the past three years — suggesting meaningful service friction. No confirmed mobile app exists for account management. These products are appropriate only for genuine short-term emergencies where the borrower has a clear repayment plan, not as ongoing credit facilities.
address: 5650 #103 E Kings Canyon Rd, Fresno, CA 93727

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee applies — this is a loan product, not a subscription. Some states may provide a statutory rescission window allowing loan cancellation within one business day. Contact provider or consult your state's lending laws for details.

certifications: State-licensed in all 27 states of operation

pros: One of the largest US non-bank short-term lenders with 800–1,200+ retail storefronts across 27 states
Same-day funding available for loan approvals submitted before 10:30am ET
Accepts applicants with bad credit or no credit history — no traditional credit score requirement
A+ BBB rating and BBB accredited as of September 2024
CFSA member, subject to industry best-practice standards for payday lenders
Nearly 30 years of operation since 1997, providing stability and regulatory track record
Dual online and in-store application channels give flexibility for borrowers who prefer in-person assistance
cons: APRs range from 143.29% to 688.28% — among the most expensive consumer credit products available
BBB customer review score is only 1.7/5 based on 154 reviews, with 302 complaints filed in the past three years
Services limited to 27 states — not available nationwide
No confirmed dedicated mobile app for account management or payments
Title loans require using your vehicle as collateral and are handled through a third-party partner (LoanCenter), not directly by Advance America
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit history who need $100–$1,500 urgently and cannot qualify for bank loans
Borrowers who need cash before their next paycheck and have a clear, short repayment timeline
People in one of the 27 served states who prefer face-to-face loan assistance at a retail storefront
Emergency-only borrowers who understand the high cost and plan to repay within the loan term
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for credit-challenged consumers facing a genuine short-term cash emergency — such as an unexpected bill or gap between paychecks — who have no viable alternative through a bank, credit union, or lower-cost lender. The main caveat is that APRs reaching 688.28% make these loans extremely expensive, and borrowers who cannot repay on schedule risk entering a costly debt cycle; these products should be treated as a last resort, not a routine financial tool.
services: Payday loans (2–4 week term, ~$15 per $100 borrowed)
Installment loans (multi-payment schedule, APRs up to 200%+)
Title loans via LoanCenter partnership (vehicle title as collateral)
In-store loan processing at 800–1,200+ retail locations
Same-day funding (approvals before 10:30am ET funded by 5pm ET)
Online account management portal (loan tracking, payments)
Loan renewals and extensions where permitted by state law

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
